steps.title: Identify the sentence's role
steps.content: The sentence moves beyond describing a problem; it proposes a remedy. Earlier sentences establish that visitor travel creates carbon emissions. This sentence suggests a way to address that: encouraging people to visit closer parks instead.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice C correctly identifies the sentence as suggesting a course of action to address the carbon emission problem described earlier. The other three miss what the sentence actually does: A treats it as presenting a new harm, B as chronicling policymaker efforts, and D as rephrasing an earlier claim. That matches choice C — C is correct.
